name: Autos & durables
description: Vehicle sales, production, inventories, and used-vehicle prices for automakers, dealers, and suppliers.

series.note: Light-vehicle-only sales rate that strips heavy trucks out of TOTALSA, the cleanest read on consumer auto demand momentum.
Class 8 heavy-truck sales are a freight-cycle and capex barometer distinct from light vehicles, driving truck OEM and component demand.
Domestically produced passenger-car sales isolate North American production demand from imports for mix and capacity analysis.
Imported-auto sales gauge import penetration and FX/tariff exposure versus domestic-built cars.
Domestic light-truck/SUV sales track the highest-margin, highest-volume U.S. segment that drives Detroit profitability.
Imported light-truck sales measure foreign-brand share gains in the dominant U.S. crossover/SUV category.
Stock of domestically produced autos on dealer/manufacturer lots; rising inventory signals incentive/discount pressure on margins.
Dollar revenue at franchised dealers and parts stores, the direct top-line for the dealer and aftermarket channel.
Timely advance estimate of dealer/parts revenue that moves dealer-group and aftermarket stocks on release.
New-vehicle transaction price inflation, a direct driver of automaker ASPs, affordability, and revenue per unit.
Wholesale/producer-level vehicle price inflation that reveals OEM pricing power and pass-through ahead of retail CPI.
Producer prices for vehicle parts, a cost-input and pricing gauge for the supplier tier and aftermarket.
Industry-level producer price index for vehicle assemblers, isolating factory-gate pricing trends for the OEM segment.
Benchmark new-auto loan APR; rising rates raise monthly payments, compress affordability, and suppress unit demand.
Outstanding auto credit at finance companies, tracking captive/lender balance-sheet growth and consumer leverage in autos.
Employment in vehicle and parts manufacturing, a labor-cost and production-activity signal for the supply side.
Non-seasonally-adjusted unit sales for actual-period volume work, calendar effects, and year-over-year unit comparisons.
